question: The line chart shows sales over the years. Which statement best describes the main difficulty in interpreting this chart?

options.A: The chart is drawn so small that it is hard to read the axis labels and judge exact changes in sales.
options.B: Sales clearly doubled every decade shown on the chart.
options.C: Sales remained exactly the same throughout the entire period.
options.D: The chart shows that sales dropped to zero midway through the period.

explanation: Sales were roughly flat from 1960 to 1970, with rises before and after. Small font sizes can make it easy to misread the flat segment as a decline.
